title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Hester visits Arthur Calgary appearing disheveled and desperate, seeking his assistance. She tells him that the peace of their lives was destroyed when he arrived and revealed a truth that shattered their sense of security. Calgary offers her a drink and listens as she describes her distress, including her romantic relationship with a doctor named Tang (Donald Craig) and her fear that he suspects her of a crime.

Hester describes a dream in which she was lost and unable to find Tang, separated from him by a bottomless pit, with Calgary on the other side reaching out to help her. Calgary assures her that he will not stand aside and intends to see the situation through. Hester explains that the group believes the perpetrator must be one of them, noting that Mr. Marshall realized a previous break-in was merely a pretense.

When Calgary asks why anyone would suspect her of killing her foster mother, Hester admits she has felt murderous impulses toward the woman due to her condescending and "high and mighty" nature. She explains that she acted out of a desire to rebel and prove her own existence, as her foster mother's presence made her feel useless and ridiculous. Calgary empathizes with her feelings of helplessness, recalling a childhood memory of wanting to get rid of a mocking headmaster. Hester expresses regret, believing that if her foster mother had lived longer, they might have eventually become friends.
